text:
Large-scale relational learning is essential for managing the vast quantities of structured data generated across domains such as computational biology, information retrieval, and natural language processing. In this paper, we introduce a novel neural network architecture designed to embed multi-relational graphs into a flexible, continuous vector space. This approach preserves and enhances the underlying structure of the original data. By training the network to capture the latent semantics of these graphs, the model learns to assign high probabilities to plausible relational components.

Our empirical evaluation demonstrates that this method achieves competitive performance in link prediction tasks on standard benchmarks and real-world knowledge bases like WordNet. Furthermore, we illustrate the scalability and versatility of our approach by applying it to word-sense disambiguation within an open-text semantic parsing framework. In this setting, the model learns to assign structured meaning representations to nearly any free-text sentence, proving it can effectively scale to datasets containing tens of thousands of nodes and thousands of unique relation types.
